betterquesting:10.desc:8:
...but they can be just as industrious as bees. These new electric companions are the perfect partners for Multiblock machines: maintenance, control, management—they can handle it all.

§6§lMaintenance§r
The §5§lDrone Centre§r is a newly developed machine. It requires no power—simply place a drone into the input bus, and it will begin operation.

Replacing the maintenance hatch on your Multiblock machines with a §lDrone Downlink Module§r will connect them to the drone network.

Once activated, the Drone Centre will automatically scan for all Downlink Modules within range and attempt to establish a connection with them. As long as the Drone Centre remains active, all connected Multiblock machines will [bold]never[/bold] suffer from annoying maintenance issues again!

[note]Goodbye, my precious 256x superconductor base ingots that were lost due to maintenance failures...[/note]


§6§lEntity Drones§r
Drones now exist as physical entities, unlocking several new features:

- When a maintenance issue occurs, a drone will deploy from the Downlink Module and perform a circular inspection flight around the affected machine.
- We've equipped drones with cameras! Click the §9§lObserve§r button in the GUI to access a real-time video feed from the drone. As long as you are in the same dimension, you can connect to the drone regardless of distance.
- While in observation mode, the WAILA HUD provides more information than normal. Additionally, aim your crosshair at a machine and press §2§lG§r to remotely open its GUI! This currently supports all GT machines and hatches. However, since drones are not equipped with robotic arms, item and fluid slots cannot be interacted with.


§6§lGroup Control & Statistics§r
The Drone Centre provides a powerful machine management interface, supporting grouping, remote observation, batch power control, and machine renaming:

- Connected machines can be organized into up to §664§r custom groups. Groups can be renamed, and a machine may belong to multiple groups.
- After selecting a group, power control actions will be applied to all machines within that group. Specifically, §6§lShift-clicking§r the shutdown button will set all machine controller covers to "Disable" mode.
- By enabling the built-in §5§lProduction Recorder§r, the Drone Centre can collect real-time statistics from all connected machines, including EU consumption, item throughput, and fluid throughput. All recorded data is stored directly inside the Drone Centre.


§6§lTiered Drones§r
[warn]Drones may crash during operation. The crash probability decreases from Tier 1 to Tier 3, and Tier 3 and Tier 4 Drones will never crash.[/warn]

Don't worry—if a backup drone is available in the input bus, the Drone Centre will consume it instantly.

Using higher-tier drones increases the operating range of the Drone Centre:

  §6•§r §l§3Drone (T1)§r
    - Range: §6128 blocks§r
    - Crash Chance: §61/28,800 per second§r (Average ~8 hours)

  §6•§r §l§3Drone (T2)§r
    - Range: §6512 blocks§r
    - Crash Chance: §61/172,800 per second§r (Average ~48 hours)

  §6•§r §l§3Drone (T3)§r
    - Range: §64,096 blocks§r
    - Crash Chance: §2Never§r

  §6•§r §l§3Drone (T4)§r
    - Range: §64,096 blocks (Automatic) / Infinite (Matching Key)§r
    - Crash Chance: §2Never§r
    - Cross-dimension: §2Yes§r


To enable cross-dimensional connections with a T4 Drone, configure matching keys on both the Drone Centre and the Downlink Module.

The Downlink Module can copy matching keys using a §lData Stick§r.

&g&#FFD700&#FF4500Our drones can now operate machines across other planets... initiating warp drive!&r


§6§lStructure§r
Refer to the NEI Structure Preview to construct the new Multiblock structure. There is also a new drone model in the centre!
[note]Hempcrete blocks may be any color![/note]
